section.heading: Authority for program

content.p: The Secretary is authorized to make grants and to provide technical assistance to public and nonprofit private entities to establish and operate transitional living youth projects for homeless youth.

section.sourceCredit: (Pub. L. 93–415, title III, § 321, as added Pub. L. 100–690, title VII, § 7273(f), Nov. 18, 1988, 102 Stat. 4455; amended Pub. L. 106–71, § 3(e), Oct. 12, 1999, 113 Stat. 1038.)

note.heading: Codification
note.p: Section was formerly classified to section 5714–1 of Title 42, The Public Health and Welfare, prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.

note.heading: Prior Provisions
note.p: A prior section 321 of Pub. L. 93–415 was renumbered section 363 and is classified to section 11275 of this title.

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 1999—Pub. L. 106–71 struck out “Purpose and” before “Authority” in section catchline and struck out subsec. (a) designation before “The Secretary” and subsec. (b) which defined “homeless youth” and “transitional living youth project”.

note.heading: Effective Date
note.p: Section effective Oct. 1, 1988, see section 7296(a) of Pub. L. 100–690, set out as an Effective Date of 1988 Amendment note under section 11101 of this title.
